description: Complete DALP platform screenshot reference, all 367 screens with explanations.

slides.title: Platform Access and Dashboard
slides.subtitle: Secure entry, navigation, live command centre.

slides.title: Secure Authentication
slides.body: The DALP login screen supports both traditional email/password authentication and passwordless passkey login via WebAuthn. This means operators and investors can authenticate using biometric credentials stored on their device, eliminating password-related security risks. Access is role-gated from the very first interaction — the platform determines available modules and permissions based on the authenticated user's assigned role before loading any content.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/01 - Login/jpg/Login.jpg

slides.title: Dashboard Overview
slides.body: The main dashboard gives operators a live command centre: portfolio valuation, AUM tracking, pending action alerts, and a blockchain activity feed. Every action performed through this screen is recorded on the immutable blockchain ledger, creating a tamper-proof audit trail that regulators, auditors, and compliance teams can rely on. The DALP platform is built on Hyperledger Besu and compatible EVM networks, providing the security guarantees of public blockchain infrastructure within a permissioned deployment model suitable for regulated financial instruments. All operations follow the configured maker-checker approval workflows, ensuring no single operator can unilaterally execute sensitive actions.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/02 - Dashboard/jpg/Dashboard 1.jpg

slides.title: Dashboard: Identity and Verification
slides.body: Reveals the identity manager module with pending verification alerts and the verification policy manager. Compliance is a first-class dashboard widget. DALP's on-chain identity system uses the ERC-3643 identity standard, storing verified claims directly on the blockchain. This means identity checks during token transfers happen in real time, without any off-chain API calls or manual verification steps. The system supports multiple claim types — KYC level, accreditation status, professional investor certification, jurisdiction — and can enforce combinations of these claims as transfer conditions, providing flexible but robust investor eligibility enforcement.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/02 - Dashboard/jpg/Dashboard 2.jpg

slides.title: Dashboard: Updated Views
slides.subtitle: New dashboard screens from March 2026 update.

slides.title: Dashboard 01: Updated View
slides.body: The updated DALP dashboard reflects the refreshed interface from the March 2026 release, with improved navigation hierarchy and a more prominent real-time asset overview panel. The command centre layout prioritises the most time-sensitive information — pending approvals, AUM movements, and blockchain events — in the top viewport so operators can assess platform status at a glance without scrolling.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24-batch2/DASHBOARD/jpg/Dashboard 01.jpg

slides.title: Dashboard 02: Updated View
slides.body: The second updated dashboard view showcases the latest portfolio summary layout, with enhanced AUM cards and a redesigned activity feed. The new layout improves information density without sacrificing readability, allowing operators to track more assets simultaneously while the live blockchain event stream keeps them informed of all platform activity in real time. The redesigned portfolio summary panel provides a consolidated view of all managed assets, enabling operators and portfolio managers to quickly assess total AUM, recent performance, and any pending events requiring attention.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24-batch2/DASHBOARD/jpg/Dashboard 02.jpg

slides.title: Dashboard 03: Updated View
slides.body: The third updated dashboard screenshot features the new monitoring widget integration and action queue visibility improvements. Operators can now see the pending action count, recent compliance events, and blockchain node status all from the main dashboard without navigating to dedicated monitoring screens, significantly reducing the time to identify and respond to operational issues.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24-batch2/DASHBOARD/jpg/Dashboard 03 .jpg

slides.title: Bond Tokenisation: Step 1
slides.body: Opening screen of the bond tokenisation wizard. Operators see a structured flow overview before beginning configuration. The guided wizard approach ensures that operators cannot skip required configuration steps or deploy an instrument with incomplete compliance settings. Each step validates its inputs before allowing progression, and the overall wizard state is preserved — operators can navigate back to review or adjust earlier steps without losing their configuration. This reduces deployment errors and ensures every tokenised instrument meets the required operational and regulatory standards before going live on-chain.

slides.title: Compliance Policy Templates
slides.body: Reusable compliance policy templates allow operators to define rules once and apply them across multiple assets. The compliance framework in DALP is modular by design — each compliance rule is an independently deployable module that can be combined with others to express complex regulatory requirements. This means issuers can configure exactly the compliance posture required for their specific instrument and jurisdiction, without being forced into a one-size-fits-all approach. Modules are enforced at the smart contract level, making them cryptographically guaranteed rather than operationally dependent.

slides.title: On-Chain Identity
slides.body: DALP on-chain identity stores verified investor credentials on the blockchain. Claims are checked automatically during transfers. DALP's on-chain identity system uses the ERC-3643 identity standard, storing verified claims directly on the blockchain. This means identity checks during token transfers happen in real time, without any off-chain API calls or manual verification steps. The system supports multiple claim types — KYC level, accreditation status, professional investor certification, jurisdiction — and can enforce combinations of these claims as transfer conditions, providing flexible but robust investor eligibility enforcement.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Detail Overview
slides.body: Post-settlement details: both legs, timestamps, wallet addresses, and on-chain transaction hashes. DALP's XvP atomic settlement mechanism uses a cryptographic commit-reveal scheme to ensure both legs of the trade settle simultaneously on-chain. This eliminates the counterparty risk inherent in traditional sequential settlement approaches, where one party delivers before the other. The atomic guarantee means that if either leg fails validation — insufficient balance, compliance check failure, or counterparty rejection — the entire settlement rolls back with no on-chain effect. This provides T+0 finality with full regulatory auditability.
